# Entify: Comparative Named Entity Recognition System
A high-performance research platform comparing CRF and spaCy NER architectures.

## Institutional Context
The project title is "Entify: Comparative Named Entity Recognition System Using CRF and spaCy Models". This was built for the Artificial Intelligence course in BSCS Semester 3 at ILM College Sargodha (UOS Affiliated), Punjab, Pakistan. Under the mentorship of Sir Abdur-Rehman, the development cycle lasted from 26 January 2026 to 26 March 2026. The system is fully operational and deployed on AWS-EC2.

## Team Structure
- **Hassan Ali (Lead Developer)**: Managed CRF lifecycle, Flask backend, and system integration.
- **Mudassir Ali (Inference Analysis)**: Handled spaCy neural pipeline analysis and inference logic.
- **Saad Ilyas (Design Systems)**: Developed the modern Glassmorphism UI using Tailwind CSS and Alpine.js.

## Technical Architecture
- **Statistical Engine**: Custom Conditional Random Fields (CRF) trained on CoNLL2003, achieving 90.32% F1. Implements marginal probability distribution for confidence scoring.
- **Neural Engine**: spaCy `en_core_web_sm` model. It utilizes an efficient CNN-based architecture (approx. 12MB) optimized for enterprise-grade low-latency inference.
- **Web Stack**: Python/Flask backend with a responsive Alpine.js/Tailwind frontend. Real-time AJAX communication for inference results.

## Key Features & Benchmarking
The platform benchmarks models across four localized Pakistani presets: News, Tech, Finance, and Business. It provides real-time tracking of:
- **Quality Score**: Heuristic calculation based on multi-word entities and label diversity.
- **Processing Latency**: Precise timing for inference across both architectures.
- **Dual Confidence**: Side-by-side probabilistic confidence scores (CRF vs SpaCy).
